content_hints.description: Wrought-iron gates topped with gilded gryphons stand ajar, one hinge twisted where something forced its way through. Blood spatter on the flagstones has already dried to a dark crust. The villa beyond is lit from within, every window blazing as if the household wants the whole ward to see that everything is fine.
content_hints.read_aloud: Wrought-iron gates topped with gilded gryphons stand ajar, one hinge twisted and bent. Dark stains on the flagstones have dried to a crust in the night air. Beyond the gate, the villa blazes with light from every window, its white facade almost too bright against the dark -- as if someone inside wants the whole ward to believe that nothing is wrong. From within, you hear the distant clash of steel.
event_triggers: On entry: Sounds of fighting echo from inside -- steel on steel, a scream cut short.

content_hints.description: A chandelier the size of a cart wheel throws fractured light across marble floors streaked with muddy bootprints. A suit of ceremonial plate armor has been knocked from its pedestal and lies in pieces near the staircase. The portraits on the walls depict generations of Gralhunds, each painted with the same sharp, acquisitive smile.
content_hints.read_aloud: A chandelier the size of a cart wheel hangs overhead, its fractured light scattering across marble floors streaked with muddy bootprints. A suit of ceremonial plate armor lies toppled near the staircase, its pieces scattered like the bones of a fallen knight. Portraits line the walls -- generations of sharp-featured nobles staring down at the chaos below with identical, painted smiles.
event_triggers: On entry: A Zhentarim thug crashes through a door to the left, bleeding from a crossbow bolt in his shoulder.

content_hints.description: The long oak table is set for twelve but dinner was never served. Soup has gone cold in porcelain bowls painted with the Gralhund crest. A crossbow bolt is embedded in the back of the host's chair. Glass crunches underfoot from a shattered wine decanter, and the carpet is stained the same deep red as the vintage it held.
content_hints.read_aloud: A long oak table is set for twelve with porcelain bowls and crystal goblets, but dinner was never served. Soup has gone cold in the bowls, and a crossbow bolt juts from the back of the host's chair. Glass crunches underfoot -- a shattered wine decanter, its contents soaking into the carpet in a spreading stain indistinguishable from blood. The candles in the centerpiece have burned to stubs, their wax pooling across the linen.

content_hints.description: Floor-to-ceiling shelves sag under the weight of leather-bound volumes and rolled scrolls. A reading desk near the window holds an open journal, its most recent entry half-finished -- the ink still wet, the quill abandoned mid-sentence. Someone left in a hurry and did not think to lock the door behind them.
content_hints.read_aloud: Floor-to-ceiling shelves sag under the weight of leather-bound volumes and rolled scrolls, filling the room with the smell of old paper and binding glue. A reading desk near the window holds an open journal, its most recent entry trailing off mid-sentence, the quill discarded beside a spreading ink blot. The chair is pushed back at an angle, as though whoever sat here stood in a hurry and never returned.

hazards.name: Glyph of Warding on desk drawer

event_triggers: On search (DC 14 Investigation): The journal reveals the Gralhunds have been playing the Zhentarim and Xanathar Guild against each other.

content_hints.description: A gallery landing overlooks the grand foyer below. Ornamental swords and shields line the walls between tall windows. One window is shattered outward, its curtain flapping in the night breeze. Muddy handprints on the balustrade suggest someone climbed up from outside -- or fled down.
content_hints.read_aloud: You step onto a gallery landing that overlooks the foyer below, its marble floor visible through the balustrade. Ornamental swords and shields hang between tall windows, one of which is shattered outward, its curtain billowing in the night breeze. Cold air pours through the gap, carrying the smell of rain-wet cobblestones. Muddy handprints smear the balustrade railing, trailing toward the broken glass.

content_hints.description: Behind a pivoting bookcase, the master bedroom's true purpose is revealed: a hidden alcove lined with lead sheeting to block divination. A small altar to Bane occupies one corner, its candles freshly lit. A locked strongbox sits beneath the altar, still warm to the touch.
content_hints.read_aloud: Beyond the pivoting bookcase, a hidden alcove opens into a space that feels wrong -- the air is too still, too warm, and sounds from outside are swallowed completely. The walls are lined with dull gray sheeting that absorbs the light. A small altar stands in one corner, its black candles burning with steady, smokeless flames that cast no flickering shadow. A locked strongbox beneath the altar radiates a faint, unnatural warmth against your fingertips.

npcs.name: Yalah Gralhund
npcs.dialogue: You have no idea what you have walked into. The Stone was never meant for you, and the powers who want it will grind this city to dust to get it back. Leave now, and I will pretend you were never here.
npcs.notes: Noble matriarch. Ice-cold pragmatist. Worships Bane in secret. Ordered the Nimblewright to steal the Stone of Golorr. Will negotiate if cornered, fight if desperate.

event_triggers: On discovery (DC 15 Perception): The lead-lined walls suggest the Gralhunds are hiding something from magical surveillance.

content_hints.description: Narrow cots line both walls of this long, low room, each with a footlocker and a hook for a uniform. Most of the beds are empty -- their occupants are either dead in the foyer or guarding upstairs. A half-eaten meal sits on a trestle table, the bread still soft.
content_hints.read_aloud: Narrow cots line both walls of this long, low room, each made with military precision. Footlockers sit at the end of every bed, and uniforms hang from hooks on the wall. Most of the beds are empty, their blankets thrown back in haste. A half-eaten meal sits on a trestle table in the center -- the bread is still soft, the ale still foaming. Whoever was here left moments ago.

npcs.name: Hurv Cossburl
npcs.dialogue: I just work here, all right? They do not pay me enough to die for them. The mechanical thing -- it went up to the roof. The mistress built it in the attic, never let any of us near it.
npcs.notes: Gralhund house guard. Terrified. Will surrender and share information about the Nimblewright if shown mercy.

content_hints.description: Potted topiaries shaped like dragons line a rooftop garden open to the Waterdeep sky. Between the planters, a mechanical figure stands motionless -- humanoid in shape but wrong in every detail. Its joints click as it turns to face you, and the moonlight catches the rapier in its articulated hand. A leather satchel hangs from its belt, bulging with something round.
content_hints.read_aloud: You emerge onto a rooftop garden open to the Waterdeep sky. Potted topiaries shaped like coiled dragons line the parapet, their leaves silvered by moonlight. Between the planters, a figure stands perfectly still -- humanoid in shape, but its proportions are wrong, its joints too many, its surface gleaming like lacquered wood. As you step closer, something clicks inside its chest, and it turns to face you with a motion that is smooth, precise, and entirely inhuman. Moonlight catches the blade in its articulated hand.
event_triggers: On combat start: The Nimblewright fights with mechanical precision, prioritizing escape over killing. If reduced to half HP, it attempts to flee across the rooftops.
On Stone recovered: The Stone of Golorr pulses with cold light and whispers a single word: 'Vault.'
